label: Moderna HQ
sub: Cambridge, MA — Moderna's global headquarters and primary mRNA R&D center; developed mRNA-1273 and broad pipeline

label: BioNTech HQ
sub: Mainz, Germany — BioNTech co-founder Ugur Sahin designed BNT162b2 here in January 2020; global oncology mRNA pipeline

label: Pfizer Vaccine R&D
sub: Pearl River, NY — Pfizer's primary vaccine research center; co-developed BNT162b2 with BioNTech

label: NIH / NIAID
sub: Bethesda, MD — National Institutes of Health; NIAID led key mRNA vaccine science including prefusion spike stabilization (Barney Graham, Kizzmekia Corbett)

label: FDA / CBER
sub: Silver Spring, MD — FDA Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research; issued EUA for BNT162b2 (Dec 2020), mRNA-1273 (Dec 2020), full approvals, and all subsequent mRNA product authorizations

label: EMA
sub: Amsterdam, Netherlands — European Medicines Agency; first jurisdiction to grant formal marketing authorization for an mRNA vaccine (Comirnaty, Dec 21, 2020)

label: WHO Geneva
sub: Geneva, Switzerland — WHO issued Emergency Use Listings for mRNA COVID vaccines; Tedros Adhanom led global vaccine equity push

label: Pfizer Kalamazoo
sub: Kalamazoo, MI — Primary US Pfizer manufacturing site for Comirnaty; helped produce hundreds of millions of doses in 2021

label: Moderna Mfg. Norwood
sub: Norwood, MA — Moderna's key US drug substance manufacturing facility; primary production site for mRNA-1273 (Spikevax) and RSV vaccine mResvia

label: BioNTech Marburg
sub: Marburg, Germany — BioNTech's largest manufacturing facility; former Novartis site repurposed for Comirnaty production; capacity of up to 750M doses/year

label: MHLW Japan
sub: Tokyo, Japan — Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare; approved Kostaive (ARCT-154), the world's first self-amplifying mRNA vaccine, on November 28, 2023

label: Serum Institute Pune
sub: Pune, India — World's largest vaccine manufacturer; developing mRNA vaccine capabilities; key node for COVAX supply and LMIC access

label: IAVI HIV Trial Site
sub: Nairobi, Kenya — Key trial site for IAVI/Moderna mRNA HIV vaccine Phase 2; high HIV incidence region; represents LMIC inclusion in mRNA vaccine research

label: Cape Town Trial Hub
sub: Cape Town, South Africa — Major site for mRNA vaccine trials in Africa including Afrigen's mRNA technology transfer hub, supported by WHO and MPP

label: Butantan Institute
sub: São Paulo, Brazil — Instituto Butantan developing mRNA vaccines in partnership with Brazilian government; Latin America's primary mRNA development hub

label: MD Anderson Cancer Center
sub: Houston, TX — Leading US oncology center enrolling patients in mRNA cancer vaccine trials including mRNA-4157/V940 Phase 3 and BNT111

label: Dana-Farber Cancer Institute
sub: Boston, MA — Key clinical trial site for personalized mRNA cancer vaccines; collaborated with Moderna on mRNA-4157 early development

label: UPenn — Karikó/Weissman Lab
sub: Philadelphia, PA — University of Pennsylvania; where Nobel laureates Katalin Karikó and Drew Weissman performed the foundational mRNA modification research (2005) enabling safe therapeutic mRNA

label: CureVac / Tübingen
sub: Tübingen, Germany — CureVac, one of the mRNA platform pioneers; early COVID vaccine (CVnCoV) failed Phase 2b/3 but company pivoted to second-gen mRNA with GSK partnership

label: Afrigen mRNA Hub
sub: Cape Town, South Africa — Afrigen Biologics; Africa's first mRNA technology hub supported by WHO and MPP; reverse-engineered Moderna's mRNA-1273 to create an open-source COVID mRNA vaccine for LMIC manufacturing

label: Memorial Sloan Kettering
sub: New York, NY — MSK Cancer Center; primary site for Phase 1 BNT122 (autogene cevumeran) personalized mRNA pancreatic cancer vaccine trial led by Dr. Vinod Balachandran; 6-year follow-up data presented at AACR 2026 show ~50% patients alive and cancer-free

label: Genentech / Roche
sub: South San Francisco, CA — Genentech (Roche subsidiary) sponsors the Phase 2 multicenter randomized trial of BNT122 personalized mRNA pancreatic cancer vaccine (NCT05968326) in partnership with BioNTech/Pfizer; one of the world's leading oncology R&D centers
